title: Labels & accessibility
description: Every input needs a label. Two ways to bind them.

questions.prompt: Which attribute connects a <label> to its <input>?
Why does clicking the label focus the input when they're linked?
options: The browser passes the click to the associated input
questions.explanation: A native browser behavior — great for a11y and mobile UX.

questions.prompt: Bind this label to the input.
The other way to associate a label with an input is:
options: Wrap the input inside the label: <label>Email <input></label>
questions.explanation: Wrapping is implicit association — for/id explicit. Both work.

questions.prompt: For a REQUIRED field, the modern signal to users is:
options: The word 'required' next to the label
An asterisk plus a legend explaining what * means
questions.explanation: * is convention, but always explain it (visually or via aria-label).

questions.prompt: placeholder is NOT a replacement for a label because:
options: It disappears when the user types
It has poor contrast by default
Screen readers may treat it inconsistently
questions.explanation: Use label for the name; placeholder for a HINT / example only.
<fieldset> is the semantic group; <legend> gives it a label.

options: Hints the browser what data to autofill (e.g. autocomplete="email")
questions.explanation: Good autocomplete values improve form UX dramatically on mobile.
